meta.name: NFR Advisor Catalog
meta.note: Relevance rules map system context to applicable NFRs. Regulations mark NFRs mandatory. Scenarios use the SEI 6-part form; measures are quantified SLOs.

regulations.name: GDPR
regulations.control: GDPR Art. 5, 25 (privacy by design), 32 (security of processing), 33 (breach notification)

regulations.name: CCPA / CPRA
regulations.full: California Consumer Privacy Act / Privacy Rights Act
regulations.control: CCPA/CPRA — notice, opt-out of sale, access & deletion rights, reasonable security

regulations.name: LGPD
regulations.full: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (Brazil)
regulations.control: LGPD — lawful basis, data subject rights, security & breach reporting

regulations.name: PIPEDA
regulations.full: Personal Information Protection & Electronic Documents Act (Canada)
regulations.control: PIPEDA — consent, safeguards, accountability

regulations.name: DPDP Act
regulations.full: India Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023
regulations.control: DPDP — consent, purpose limitation, security safeguards, breach notice

regulations.name: PCI-DSS
regulations.full: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard v4.0
regulations.control: PCI-DSS v4.0 Req 3 (protect stored data), 4 (encrypt transit), 8 (auth), 10 (logging), 6 (patch)

regulations.name: SOX
regulations.control: SOX §404 — controls over financial reporting; change management, segregation of duties, audit trail

regulations.name: PSD2 / SCA
regulations.full: EU Payment Services Directive 2 (Strong Customer Auth)
regulations.control: PSD2 — strong customer authentication, secure open-banking APIs, transaction integrity

regulations.name: MiFID II
regulations.full: EU Markets in Financial Instruments Directive II
regulations.control: MiFID II — transaction recording/reporting, clock sync, record retention, best execution

regulations.name: DORA
regulations.control: DORA — ICT risk management, incident reporting, resilience testing, third-party risk

regulations.name: HIPAA
regulations.full: US Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
regulations.control: HIPAA Security Rule §164.312 — access control, audit, integrity, transmission security

regulations.name: HITECH
regulations.full: US Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act
regulations.control: HITECH — breach notification, audit, encryption safe harbour

regulations.name: FDA 21 CFR Part 11
regulations.control: 21 CFR Part 11 — validated systems, audit trails, electronic signatures, record integrity

regulations.name: IEC 62304 / MDR
regulations.full: Medical Device Software lifecycle & EU MDR
regulations.control: IEC 62304 — software safety classification, risk management, V&V; EU MDR conformity

regulations.name: EU AI Act
regulations.control: EU AI Act — risk classification, transparency, human oversight, data governance, logging for high-risk AI

regulations.name: ISO 42001
regulations.control: ISO/IEC 42001 — AI governance, risk & impact assessment, lifecycle controls

regulations.name: NIST AI RMF
regulations.control: NIST AI RMF — Govern, Map, Measure, Manage; trustworthiness characteristics

regulations.name: NIST CSF 2.0
regulations.control: NIST CSF — Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover

regulations.name: ISO 27001
regulations.control: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A — A.5 policies, A.8 access, A.12 operations, A.17 continuity

regulations.name: SOC 2
regulations.control: SOC 2 TSC — Security (CC), Availability (A1), Confidentiality (C1), Processing Integrity (PI1)

regulations.name: OWASP ASVS
regulations.control: OWASP ASVS — authentication, session, access control, validation, logging verification levels

regulations.name: FedRAMP
regulations.full: US Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program
regulations.control: FedRAMP (NIST 800-53 baselines) — cloud authorization, continuous monitoring

regulations.name: WCAG / EAA
regulations.control: WCAG 2.2 AA conformance; EU EAA obligations for consumer products/services

regulations.name: ADA / Section 508
regulations.full: US Americans with Disabilities Act & Section 508
regulations.control: ADA Title III / Section 508 — accessible digital services (WCAG-aligned)

regulations.name: ISO 22301
regulations.control: ISO 22301 — BCM policy, BIA, continuity strategies, exercising & testing

nfrs.name: Reusability
nfrs.businessImpact: Duplicated logic across teams inflates cost and lets defects diverge; low reuse slows delivery.

qa.stimulus: needs a capability an existing module already provides
qa.response: they consume it via a published interface instead of forking or copy-paste
qa.measure: Reused through a versioned, published interface; code duplication < 5%

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Duplication scanner under threshold; shared components consumed via versioned packages

nfrs.name: Analysability / Diagnosability
nfrs.businessImpact: If engineers can't quickly locate why something failed, incidents drag on and MTTR balloons.

qa.stimulus: investigates a production defect
qa.response: they isolate the root cause from telemetry without guesswork
qa.measure: Root cause located < 30 min from existing logs/traces; > 90% of code paths traceable

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Trace/log coverage measured; sample incidents diagnosed within target from telemetry alone

nfrs.name: Installability & Replaceability
nfrs.businessImpact: Hard-to-install or hard-to-replace components create lock-in and make new environments or vendor swaps costly.
metrics: time to install in a fresh env

qa.stimulus: installs, upgrades, or replaces the component
qa.artifact: the deployable artifact and its config
qa.response: it installs cleanly and is replaceable by a conformant alternative with no code change
qa.measure: Installs in a fresh environment < 30 min; replaceable behind a stable interface with zero code change

nfrs.fitnessFunction: One-command install in a clean environment passes; component swapped behind its interface in a test

nfrs.name: Co-existence
nfrs.businessImpact: A component that hogs shared resources or interferes with neighbors degrades unrelated systems and erodes trust in shared platforms.

qa.stimulus: runs concurrently on shared infrastructure
qa.artifact: the component and its resource footprint
qa.response: it stays within its quotas and does not degrade neighbors
qa.measure: Honors CPU/memory/IO quotas; zero cross-tenant interference incidents

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Resource limits enforced; contention load test confirms no neighbor degradation

nfrs.name: UI Consistency & Aesthetics
nfrs.businessImpact: Inconsistent, unpolished UI raises cognitive load, hurts brand trust, and increases support load.

qa.stimulus: moves across screens and flows
qa.response: controls, patterns, and styling are consistent and self-explanatory
qa.measure: 100% of components from the design system; SUS > 75; zero critical consistency defects

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Visual-regression suite green; design-system adoption measured; SUS above target

nfrs.name: Low Latency / Responsiveness
nfrs.businessImpact: Slow responses cut conversion and engagement; in trading, latency directly loses money.

qa.source: An end user under normal interactive load
qa.stimulus: submits a request
qa.response: the system returns a complete response

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Load test asserts p95 < target under N concurrent users (k6/Gatling in CI)

nfrs.name: Throughput
nfrs.businessImpact: Insufficient throughput causes queue backlogs and timeouts at peak, dropping revenue and SLAs.

qa.stimulus: peak load arrives (e.g. flash sale, market open)
qa.response: requests are processed without queue growth
qa.measure: Sustains target TPS at < SLA latency for the event window

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Soak test sustains target TPS for 1h without degradation

nfrs.name: Scalability
nfrs.businessImpact: Without elastic scale, growth or spikes force outages or expensive over-provisioning.

qa.stimulus: load doubles within an hour
qa.response: capacity scales out automatically
qa.measure: No SLO breach; scale-out completes < 5 min

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Scale test: 2x load triggers auto-scale within bound, SLO holds

nfrs.name: Availability
nfrs.businessImpact: Downtime stops revenue, breaches SLAs, and erodes trust; regulated sectors face penalties.

qa.stimulus: a node or availability zone fails
qa.response: traffic fails over with no user-visible outage
qa.measure: Meets availability SLO; e.g. 99.99% = ≤ 52.6 min/yr downtime

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Game-day: kill an instance/AZ, verify SLO and error budget hold

nfrs.name: Fault Tolerance / Resilience
nfrs.businessImpact: A single dependency failure cascading into a full outage multiplies incident cost and blast radius.

qa.stimulus: times out or returns errors
qa.response: the failure is isolated and the system degrades gracefully
qa.measure: Core flows stay up; no cascading failure; < 1% error amplification

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Fault-injection (chaos) test confirms no cascading failure

nfrs.name: Recoverability / DR
nfrs.businessImpact: Inability to recover from data loss or regional failure is an existential and often regulatory risk.

qa.stimulus: a region or datastore is lost
qa.response: service is restored from backups/replicas
qa.measure: RTO < 1h, RPO < 5 min (tier-dependent)

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Quarterly restore drill meets RTO/RPO

nfrs.name: Confidentiality
nfrs.businessImpact: A breach of sensitive data means regulatory fines, breach notification, and lasting reputational damage.

qa.stimulus: intercepts traffic or gains storage access
qa.response: data remains unintelligible / inaccessible
qa.measure: 100% sensitive data encrypted (TLS 1.3, AES-256); zero plaintext exposure

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Scanner verifies no plaintext sensitive data; TLS enforced; secrets not in code

nfrs.name: Integrity
nfrs.businessImpact: Undetected tampering corrupts records and decisions; in finance/health it causes direct harm and liability.

qa.stimulus: attempts to tamper with data in transit or at rest
qa.response: the change is rejected or detected
qa.measure: All writes authorized & validated; tampering detectable 100% of the time

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Security test: tampered payloads are rejected; checksums verified

nfrs.name: Authentication & Authorization
nfrs.businessImpact: Broken access control is the #1 OWASP risk; it enables data theft and privilege escalation.

qa.stimulus: requests a protected resource
qa.response: access granted only with valid identity + permission
qa.measure: 100% protected endpoints enforce authz; MFA on all privileged access

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Auth tests: unauthenticated/forbidden requests return 401/403; no IDOR

nfrs.name: Auditability / Accountability
nfrs.businessImpact: Without an immutable trail you fail audits, can't investigate incidents, and can't prove compliance.

qa.stimulus: investigates an action or access
qa.response: a complete, immutable, attributable trail is available
qa.measure: Every sensitive action attributable to an identity; logs retained per policy (e.g. 1yr)

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Verify sensitive actions produce immutable, attributable audit entries

nfrs.name: Modularity
nfrs.businessImpact: High coupling slows every future change and raises the risk and cost of each release.

qa.stimulus: changes one module
qa.response: no unrelated module needs modification
qa.measure: Change isolated to one module; zero new dependency cycles

nfrs.fitnessFunction: ArchUnit / dependency-cruiser: no forbidden dependencies or cycles

nfrs.name: Testability
nfrs.businessImpact: Low testability means slow, risky releases and escaped defects reaching production.

qa.stimulus: commits a change
qa.response: automated tests verify behaviour
qa.measure: Critical paths covered; CI gate enforced at agreed threshold

nfrs.fitnessFunction: CI fails below coverage threshold on critical paths

nfrs.name: Modifiability / Evolvability
nfrs.businessImpact: Slow time-to-market for changes loses competitive ground and frustrates the business.

qa.stimulus: requests a new business rule
qa.response: it is implemented and shipped quickly and safely
qa.measure: Lead time within target (e.g. < 1 week); change-failure rate < 15%

nfrs.fitnessFunction: DORA metrics tracked; lead-time and CFR within SLO

nfrs.name: Accessibility
nfrs.businessImpact: Inaccessible products exclude users and expose the org to legal action (ADA, EAA).

qa.source: A user relying on assistive technology
qa.stimulus: navigates a core journey
qa.response: all functions are operable
qa.measure: WCAG 2.2 AA on all critical journeys; zero blocker violations

nfrs.fitnessFunction: axe-core CI scan passes on key pages

nfrs.name: Learnability / Ease of Use
nfrs.businessImpact: A confusing product raises support cost and abandonment and depresses adoption.

qa.stimulus: attempts a core task unaided
qa.response: they complete it without help
qa.measure: Task completion > 90%; time-to-first-success < target

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Usability test: target completion rate met

nfrs.name: Interoperability
nfrs.businessImpact: Poor interoperability blocks partnerships and integrations and creates costly custom glue.

qa.stimulus: integrates via the API
qa.response: data is exchanged correctly using standard contracts
qa.measure: Conforms to published schema; 100% contract tests green

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Contract tests against published schema pass

nfrs.name: Portability / Deployability
nfrs.businessImpact: Vendor lock-in and manual deploys raise switching costs and slow delivery.

qa.stimulus: must run the system in a new environment
qa.response: it deploys with config-only changes
qa.measure: Deploys to a new environment with zero code change

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Pipeline deploys identical artifact to 2+ environments

nfrs.name: Observability
nfrs.businessImpact: Blind operations mean long outages (high MTTR), missed SLAs, and firefighting instead of prevention.

qa.stimulus: an incident occurs in production
qa.response: operators locate the root cause quickly
qa.measure: MTTD < 5 min; golden signals dashboarded; SLO-based alerts

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Every service emits the three pillars; alerts tied to SLOs

nfrs.name: Cost Efficiency / FinOps
nfrs.businessImpact: Runaway cloud spend erodes margin; uncontrolled unit economics make growth unprofitable.

qa.stimulus: traffic drops off-peak
qa.response: resource cost scales down proportionally
qa.measure: Cost-per-transaction within budget; < 10% idle waste

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Cost-per-transaction tracked; budget alert configured

nfrs.name: Data Consistency
nfrs.businessImpact: Lost updates or stale reads cause financial errors, double-spends, and incorrect decisions.

qa.stimulus: issue concurrent writes to the same data
qa.response: the chosen consistency guarantee is preserved
qa.measure: No lost updates; staleness within declared bound (per aggregate)

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Concurrency test: no lost updates beyond declared model

nfrs.name: Privacy / Data Protection
nfrs.businessImpact: Privacy failures trigger regulatory fines (up to 4% of global turnover under GDPR) and loss of user trust.

qa.stimulus: requests access, deletion, or consent withdrawal
qa.response: the request is honoured and data handling is lawful by design
qa.measure: DSAR fulfilled within statutory window; data minimized; residency enforced

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Verify retention enforced, DSAR workflow works, residency honoured

nfrs.name: Sustainability / Green IT
nfrs.businessImpact: Carbon and energy reporting is increasingly mandated (CSRD); efficiency also lowers cost.

qa.stimulus: processes workload
qa.response: work is done with minimal energy/carbon
qa.measure: Software Carbon Intensity within target; demand shifted to low-carbon windows/regions

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Track SCI per deployment; alert on regressions

nfrs.name: Supply-Chain Security
nfrs.businessImpact: Compromised dependencies or build pipelines (e.g. SolarWinds, Log4Shell) cause systemic breaches.
metrics: mean time to patch critical CVE

qa.source: A compromised dependency or build step
qa.stimulus: a malicious or vulnerable component enters the pipeline
qa.response: it is detected and blocked before release
qa.measure: SBOM generated each build; critical CVEs patched < 7 days; signed artifacts

nfrs.fitnessFunction: CI blocks builds with critical CVEs / unsigned artifacts; SBOM attached

nfrs.name: Safety
nfrs.businessImpact: In physical, medical, or industrial systems, failures can injure people — the highest-stakes NFR.

qa.stimulus: drives the system toward a hazardous state
qa.response: the system enters a safe state
qa.measure: All identified hazards mitigated; fail-safe within reaction-time budget

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Hazard scenarios tested; safe-state transition verified within time budget

nfrs.name: Data Quality
nfrs.businessImpact: Bad data drives bad decisions, broken reports, and (for ML) degraded models — garbage in, garbage out.

qa.stimulus: ingests a batch/stream of records
qa.response: invalid or stale data is detected and quarantined
qa.measure: > 99% records pass validation; freshness within SLA; anomalies alerted

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Pipeline gates on validation; freshness/volume anomalies alert

nfrs.name: Localization / Internationalization
nfrs.businessImpact: Lack of localization caps addressable market and harms UX in non-primary locales.

qa.source: A user in a different locale
qa.stimulus: uses the product in their language/format
qa.response: language, dates, currency, and layout adapt correctly
qa.measure: Target locales fully supported; 100% strings externalized; RTL where needed

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Pseudo-localization test passes; no hard-coded strings

nfrs.name: Configurability
nfrs.businessImpact: Hard-coded behaviour forces a deploy for every tweak; configurability enables safe, fast change and multi-tenancy.

qa.stimulus: needs to change behaviour or branding
qa.response: the change applies without a code deploy
qa.measure: Targeted behaviours changeable via config/flags with audit + rollback

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Flag flip changes behaviour without redeploy; changes audited

nfrs.name: Backward / API Compatibility
nfrs.businessImpact: Breaking changes strand partner integrations and clients, causing outages outside your control.

qa.stimulus: a new version of the service is released
qa.response: existing clients keep working unchanged
qa.measure: No breaking change without versioning + deprecation window; contract tests green

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Contract/compat tests fail the build on a breaking change

nfrs.name: AI Explainability & Transparency
nfrs.businessImpact: Opaque AI decisions fail EU AI Act / sector rules, block adoption, and create legal exposure for automated decisions.

qa.stimulus: questions an AI-driven decision
qa.response: a human-understandable explanation is produced
qa.measure: Every consequential decision explainable; model cards & lineage maintained

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Each consequential decision emits an explanation artifact; model card present

nfrs.name: AI Robustness & Drift Management
nfrs.businessImpact: Models silently degrade as data shifts; undetected drift erodes accuracy and trust and can cause harmful outputs.

qa.stimulus: input distribution shifts or is adversarial
qa.response: degradation is detected and the model is retrained/rolled back
qa.measure: Drift detected < 1 day; accuracy stays within tolerance; guardrails block unsafe output

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Drift monitors alert on threshold; canary gates model promotion

nfrs.name: Secrets Management
nfrs.businessImpact: Leaked credentials and keys are a top breach vector; poor rotation turns one leak into a permanent one.

qa.stimulus: needs / attempts to obtain a credential
qa.response: secrets are vaulted, scoped, and rotated; none in code
qa.measure: 100% secrets in a vault; rotation ≤ 90 days; zero secrets in source

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Secret scanner blocks commits; no static long-lived keys

nfrs.name: Vulnerability & Patch Management
nfrs.businessImpact: Unpatched known CVEs are exploited within days; slow patching is a leading cause of breaches.
metrics: mean time to patch critical CVE

qa.stimulus: a critical CVE is published for a dependency
qa.response: affected components are patched or mitigated
qa.measure: Critical CVEs remediated < 7 days; no end-of-life components in prod

nfrs.fitnessFunction: CI fails on critical CVEs; estate scanned continuously

nfrs.name: Session Management
nfrs.businessImpact: Weak sessions enable hijacking and fixation, leading to account takeover.

qa.stimulus: attempts to hijack or fixate a session
qa.response: the session is protected and invalidated appropriately
qa.measure: Secure/HttpOnly/SameSite cookies; idle & absolute timeouts; rotation on privilege change

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Auth tests verify session timeout, flags, and revocation

nfrs.name: Rate Limiting & Abuse Prevention
nfrs.businessImpact: Without throttling, scraping, credential stuffing, and DDoS degrade or take down public services.

qa.stimulus: floods endpoints with requests
qa.response: excess traffic is throttled or blocked
qa.measure: All public endpoints rate-limited; volumetric attacks absorbed by edge protection

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Load/abuse test confirms limits enforced; edge protection active

nfrs.name: Non-Repudiation
nfrs.businessImpact: Disputed transactions without proof create legal and financial liability, especially in finance/health.

qa.stimulus: later disputes having performed an action
qa.response: cryptographic proof of the action and actor is produced
qa.measure: All legally-significant actions signed & timestamped; proof verifiable

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Verify signed records are produced and independently verifiable

nfrs.name: Data Durability
nfrs.businessImpact: Losing committed data is often unrecoverable trust and revenue damage, and may breach regulation.

qa.stimulus: a disk, node, or zone fails after a write is acknowledged
qa.response: acknowledged data is never lost
qa.measure: ≥ 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability; zero acknowledged-write loss

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Kill-a-node test after ack confirms no data loss

nfrs.name: Capacity Planning & Headroom
nfrs.businessImpact: Running without headroom causes peak-time outages; over-provisioning wastes budget.

qa.stimulus: demand approaches provisioned capacity
qa.response: capacity is provisioned ahead of need
qa.measure: ≥ 30% headroom at forecast peak; no capacity-driven incidents

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Forecast vs actual tracked; headroom alerting in place

nfrs.name: Data Retention & Lifecycle
nfrs.businessImpact: Keeping data too long breaches privacy law; deleting too soon breaches record-keeping law.

qa.stimulus: data reaches end-of-life or erasure is requested
qa.response: data is retained, archived, or deleted per policy
qa.measure: Every data class has a retention rule; erasure honoured within statutory window

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Verify retention rules enforced; erasure workflow tested

nfrs.name: Data Lineage & Traceability
nfrs.businessImpact: Without lineage you can't trust reports, debug pipelines, or prove how a figure was derived.

qa.stimulus: questions where a data value came from
qa.response: the full upstream lineage is shown
qa.measure: End-to-end lineage available for all governed datasets

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Lineage graph generated and queryable for governed datasets

nfrs.name: Requirements Traceability
nfrs.businessImpact: In regulated/safety domains, inability to trace requirement → design → test → evidence fails audits.

qa.stimulus: asks for evidence a requirement is implemented & verified
qa.response: the requirement is traced to design, code, and a passing test
qa.measure: 100% safety/compliance requirements traced to verification evidence

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Traceability report shows no untested critical requirement

nfrs.name: Supportability & Operability
nfrs.businessImpact: Poorly supportable systems mean long incidents, key-person risk, and high operational cost.

qa.stimulus: responds to an alert at 3am
qa.response: they diagnose and remediate using documented procedures
qa.measure: Every alert links a runbook; common ops automated; docs current

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Each alert maps to a tested runbook; docs reviewed each release

nfrs.name: Deployability & Release Safety
nfrs.businessImpact: Slow, risky deploys throttle delivery and make every release a high-stress event with rollback risk.

qa.stimulus: ships a change to production
qa.response: the change deploys safely with fast rollback if needed
qa.measure: On-demand deploys; CFR < 15%; rollback < 5 min (DORA elite/high)

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Pipeline enforces gates; canary + automated rollback verified

nfrs.name: Error Prevention & Recovery (UX)
nfrs.businessImpact: Poor error handling causes user mistakes, lost work, and support load; it erodes trust.

qa.stimulus: makes a mistake or hits an error
qa.response: the system prevents, explains, and helps recover from the error
qa.measure: Destructive actions confirmable/undoable; clear messages; no dead ends

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Usability test: users recover from injected errors without support

nfrs.name: AI Fairness & Bias Mitigation
nfrs.businessImpact: Biased AI causes discriminatory outcomes — legal liability, regulatory action, and reputational harm.

qa.stimulus: outcomes are evaluated across demographic groups
qa.response: outcomes are equitable within tolerance
qa.measure: Disparate-impact ratio within policy bounds; fairness tracked per release

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Fairness suite gates model promotion; production parity monitored

nfrs.name: AI Human Oversight & Control
nfrs.businessImpact: Unsupervised high-impact AI decisions are a regulatory red line (EU AI Act) and a safety risk.

qa.stimulus: an AI makes a consequential decision
qa.response: a human can review, override, or halt the decision
qa.measure: All high-impact decisions support human review/override; kill-switch available

nfrs.fitnessFunction: Verify override path and kill-switch work for high-impact decisions
